{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether natalizumab improves the disease course of secondary progressive multiple sclerosis (SPMS). Reviewing the documents, natalizumab is FDA-approved for treating multiple sclerosis, primarily in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS), where it has been shown to reduce relapse rates and slow symptom progression during flares. For example, Document [4] states that natalizumab reduces relapse rates in RRMS, with 77% of patients being relapse-free at one year compared to 56% on placebo. Document [0] and [11] confirm that natalizumab is approved for multiple sclerosis but note that its use is primarily in RRMS, not in secondary progressive MS. Document [3] and [13] describe SPMS as a steady decline in neurological function, with neurodegenerative processes being central. There is no evidence in the provided documents indicating that natalizumab has been studied or shown to improve disease progression in secondary progressive multiple sclerosis. The available data focus on relapsing-remitting MS, not secondary progressive MS. Therefore, based on the evidence, natalizumab does not improve the disease course of secondary progressive multiple sclerosis.", "answer_choice": "B"}
